what: NS-IBTS Q1 — North Sea demersal survey
title: Catch products: from raw exchange data to survey indices
text: Species absent many hauls. Dropping zeros inflates mean CPUE makes trends unreliable. dr_catch_by_haul() adds explicit zero row every haul species caught (within Survey/Year/Quarter stratum). Goatfish (Mullus surmuletus) good illustration: appears virtually every year since late 1990s caught ~13 % hauls, present arrives schools giving high CPUE. Ignoring zero hauls overstates mean roughly 7-fold. Atlantic cod shows bias, recent decades stock declined hauls cod became norm.  bubble plot haul positions shows species concentrated given year. Atlantic cod 2015; circle area proportional numbers caught per haul (zero hauls shown small open circles).  pipeline limited single quarter. Passing Q1 Q3 NS-IBTS one call shows year-class shifts rightward within year fish grow, across years. Three years bracketing strong 2000 year-class illustrate clearly — within year Q3 fish noticeably larger Q1 fish, dominant cohort advances ~10 cm per year.  Length-based indices require zero-filling length level aggregating — otherwise hauls small fish caught silently dropped. dr_expand_length() ensures every haul contributes, including zero juveniles. Haddock spawn spring (March–May). NS-IBTS runs Q1 (January–March) Q3 (July–September), giving two windows onto year-classes different ages: Q3 ≤ 15 cm: 0-group fish (~3–4 months old), representing year-class spawned spring (year-class Y seen Q3 year Y). Q1 ≤ 22 cm: 1-group fish (~10 months old), representing year-class spawned previous spring (year-class Y−1 seen Q1 year Y). Aligning year-class puts axis provides independent cross-check year-class strength.  dr_standardize_hl() carries p_females column — proportion female among sexed individuals length (see Standardized HL formula NA semantics). Splitting n_haul/n_hour female male components p_females, zero-filling component separately dr_expand_length(), gives sex-specific length index — sex treated number--length series, existing zero-fill machinery applies unchanged. Nephrops norvegicus (Dublin Bay lobster) good candidate: reliably sexed sampling, p_females non-missing 90% NS-IBTS length records. p_females-derived counts assume catch fully sexed n_haul * p_females equals true female count essentially individuals length/haul sexed ("B"/"U"/unsexed catch). Nephrops, > 90% sexing coverage, safe approximation. species recorded substantial unsexed bycatch, treat derived counts indicative, exact.  Female male numbers track closely ~4 cm, female numbers fall sharply male numbers persist larger size classes — males reach larger sizes females species.
code: species_ns <- c("goatfish", "Atlantic cod")  cpue_ns <- cbl |>   filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter == 1, species %in% species_ns) |>   dr_catch_by_haul(hh) |>   collect()  bind_rows(   cpue_ns                      |> mutate(zeros = "with zero hauls"),   cpue_ns |> filter(n_hour > 0) |> mutate(zeros = "without zero hauls") ) |>   ggplot(aes(Year, n_hour, colour = zeros)) +   stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "pointrange", size = 0.3) +   facet_wrap(~species, scales = "free_y") +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("with zero hauls"    = "#2166ac",                                  "without zero hauls" = "#d73027")) +   labs(y = "Numbers per hour (mean ± 95 % boot CI)",        x = NULL, colour = NULL,        title = "NS-IBTS Q1 — effect of zero-haul inclusion on CPUE") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") cod_hauls_2015 <- cbl |>   filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter == 1,          species == "Atlantic cod", Year == 2015) |>   dr_catch_by_haul(hh) |>   left_join(select(hh, .id, ShootLongitude, ShootLatitude), by = ".id") |>   collect()  ggplot(cod_hauls_2015,        aes(ShootLongitude, ShootLatitude)) +   geom_sf(data = dr_coastline, inherit.aes = FALSE,           fill = "grey85", colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.2) +   geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, n_haul == 0),              shape = 1, size = 1, colour = "grey40") +   geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, n_haul > 0),              aes(size = n_haul), colour = "#2166ac", alpha = 0.6) +   scale_size_area(max_size = 10, name = "Numbers per haul") +   coord_sf(xlim = c(-4, 13), ylim = c(49, 62)) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,        title = "NS-IBTS Q1 2015 — Atlantic cod catch per haul") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "right") cbl |>   filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter %in% c(1, 3), species == "haddock",          Year %in% 2000:2002) |>   dr_expand_length(hh) |>   collect() |>   mutate(YQ = paste0(Year, "-", Quarter)) |>   # mutate(Quarter = paste0("Q", Quarter)) |>   ggplot(aes(length_cm, n_hour)) +   stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "ribbon",                aes(ymin = after_stat(ymin), ymax = after_stat(ymax)),                alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line") +   #scale_fill_manual(values   = c(Q1 = "#2166ac", Q3 = "#d73027")) +   #scale_colour_manual(values = c(Q1 = "#2166ac", Q3 = "#d73027")) +   facet_grid(YQ ~ .) +   labs(x = "Length (cm)", y = "Numbers per hour (mean ± 95 % boot CI)",        fill = NULL, colour = NULL,        title = "NS-IBTS Q1 & Q3 — haddock length distribution 2000–2002") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(5, 45)) cbl |>   filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter %in% c(1, 3), species == "haddock") |>   dr_expand_length(hh) |>   collect() |>   filter((Quarter == 1 & length_cm <= 22) | (Quarter == 3 & length_cm <= 15)) |>   mutate(     year_class = if_else(Quarter == 1, Year - 1L, Year),     quarter_label = if_else(Quarter == 1,                             "Q1 (<= 22 cm, age ~10 months)",                             "Q3 (<= 15 cm, age ~4 months)")   ) |>   group_by(year_class, quarter_label, .id) |>   summarise(n_hour = sum(n_hour), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(year_class, n_hour, colour = quarter_label)) +   stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "pointrange",                size = 0.3, position = position_dodge(0.4)) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("Q1 (<= 22 cm, age ~10 months)" = "#2166ac",                                  "Q3 (<= 15 cm, age ~4 months)"  = "#d73027")) +   labs(y = "Numbers per hour (mean ± 95 % boot CI)",        x = "Year-class", colour = NULL,        title = "NS-IBTS — haddock year-class strength index") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") nephrops_2015 <- cbl |>   filter(species == "Dublin Bay lobster",          Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter == 1, Year == 2015,          # carapace length in cm; a minority of records use a different          # length convention (likely total length) and are excluded here          length_cm <= 6, !is.na(p_females)) |>   collect() |>   mutate(n_females = n_haul * p_females, n_males = n_haul * (1 - p_females),          h_females = n_hour * p_females, h_males   = n_hour * (1 - p_females))  hh_2015 <- hh |>   filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter == 1, Year == 2015, HaulValidity == "V") |>   collect()  female_lf <- nephrops_2015 |>   mutate(n_haul = n_females, n_hour = h_females) |>   select(.id, Survey, Year, Quarter, aphia, latin, species,          length_mm, length_cm, accuracy, n_haul, n_hour, SpeciesValidity) |>   dr_expand_length(hh_2015) |>   mutate(sex = "female")  male_lf <- nephrops_2015 |>   mutate(n_haul = n_males, n_hour = h_males) |>   select(.id, Survey, Year, Quarter, aphia, latin, species,          length_mm, length_cm, accuracy, n_haul, n_hour, SpeciesValidity) |>   dr_expand_length(hh_2015) |>   mutate(sex = "male")  bind_rows(female_lf, male_lf) |>   group_by(sex, length_cm) |>   summarise(mean_n_hour = mean(n_hour, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(length_cm, mean_n_hour, colour = sex)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(female = "#d73027", male = "#2166ac")) +   labs(x = "Carapace length (cm)", y = "Numbers per hour (mean)",        colour = NULL,        title = "NS-IBTS Q1 2015 — Nephrops norvegicus length index by sex") +   theme_bw()

title: Catch products: from raw exchange data to survey indices
text: Length-based indices require zero-filling length level aggregating — otherwise hauls small fish caught silently dropped. dr_expand_length() ensures every haul contributes, including zero juveniles. Haddock spawn spring (March–May). NS-IBTS runs Q1 (January–March) Q3 (July–September), giving two windows onto year-classes different ages: Q3 ≤ 15 cm: 0-group fish (~3–4 months old), representing year-class spawned spring (year-class Y seen Q3 year Y). Q1 ≤ 22 cm: 1-group fish (~10 months old), representing year-class spawned previous spring (year-class Y−1 seen Q1 year Y). Aligning year-class puts axis provides independent cross-check year-class strength.

title: Catch products: from raw exchange data to survey indices
text: BTS covers Dutch, Belgian English coastal waters summer. Common dab plaice dominate; survey also records seahorses consistently.  Seahorses (Hippocampus spp.) rarely caught occurrence trawl surveys used indicator seagrass habitat condition. BTS consistent records DATRAS.
code: species_bts <- c("common dab", "European plaice", "common sole")  cbl |>   filter(Survey == "BTS", Quarter == 3, species %in% species_bts) |>   dr_catch_by_haul(hh) |>   collect() |>   ggplot(aes(Year, n_hour)) +   stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "pointrange",                colour = "#1b7837", size = 0.3) +   facet_wrap(~species, scales = "free_y") +   labs(y = "Numbers per hour (mean ± 95 % boot CI)",        x = NULL,        title = "BTS Q3 — flatfish CPUE indices") +   theme_bw() seahorse_aphia <- cbl |>   filter(Survey == "BTS", grepl("Hippocampus", latin)) |>   distinct(aphia, latin, species) |>   collect()  cbl |>   filter(Survey == "BTS", aphia %in% !!seahorse_aphia$aphia) |>   dr_catch_by_haul(hh) |>   collect() |>   mutate(present = n_hour > 0) |>   group_by(Year, species) |>   summarise(prevalence = mean(present),             mean_cpue  = mean(n_hour),             .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(Year)) +   geom_col(aes(y = prevalence), fill = "#c2a5cf", alpha = 0.7) +   geom_line(aes(y = mean_cpue / max(mean_cpue) * max(prevalence)),             colour = "#762a83", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~species) +   scale_y_continuous(     name = "Proportion of hauls with catch (bars)",     sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . * 1, name = "Scaled mean CPUE (line)")   ) +   labs(x = NULL,        title = "BTS Q3 — seahorse prevalence and relative CPUE") +   theme_bw()

what: A note on dr_expand_length() and large selections
title: Catch products: from raw exchange data to survey indices
text: dr_expand_length() builds complete haul × species × length-bin grid, output size grows Nhauls×Nspecies×Nlength binsN_\text{hauls} \times N_\text{species} \times N_\text{length bins}. Even , DuckDB backend handles surprisingly large jobs efficiently. Running pipeline species NS-IBTS Q1 — every year, every species — takes tens seconds produces millions rows. real limit R memory: lazy query always safe, collect() across multiple surveys simultaneously may exceed available RAM. use dr_catch_by_haul() vs dr_expand_length() Use dr_catch_by_haul() need haul-level totals — skips length grid entirely substantially faster. Reserve dr_expand_length() analyses genuinely need length structure: length-based indices, year-class tracking, age-length keys.
code: t10 <- system.time(   res10 <- cbl |>     filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter == 1, species %in% species_10) |>     dr_expand_length(hh) |>     collect() )  cat(sprintf("10 species: %s rows — %.1f s\n",             format(nrow(res10), big.mark = ","), t10["elapsed"])) 10 species: 14,677,149 rows — 13.2 s t_all <- system.time(   res_all <- cbl |>     filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter == 1) |>     dr_expand_length(hh) |>     collect() )  cat(sprintf("all species: %s rows — %.1f s\n",             format(nrow(res_all), big.mark = ","), t_all["elapsed"]))

title: Parquet Files and DuckDB
text: CSV stores one row time: Parquet stores one column time: Survey values together, Year values together, . sounds like minor implementation detail, consequence important: ask Survey Year, Parquet reads two columns disk. 28 columns never touched. Every Parquet file carries footer — compact block binary metadata end file. footer contains: column names column types (INT32, UTF8, DOUBLE, …) number rows row-group boundaries (file split chunks; footer says starts) min/max statistics per chunk, per column DuckDB first connects Parquet file reads footer — kilobytes even file millions rows. knows schema, row count, chunks can skip entirely filter applied. Types recorded file enforced read. INT32 column comes back integer, UTF8 column comes back character — guessing, surprises. reading XML source (dr_get(source = "xml")), parquet file involved ; types applied separately dr_settypes() using dr_lookup_fields table reference. Either way result correctly typed data frame, two paths independent. Within column chunk, values compressed. Parquet supports several algorithms; obus server files use Snappy, fast algorithm optimised decompression speed. Similar values compress extremely well: column string "NS-IBTS" repeated 500 000 times compresses almost nothing. Numeric columns many repeated values (like Quarter, 1–4) shrink similarly. result, measured directly server file freshly written CSV data: full HL table (14,397,334 rows, 30 columns) occupies 97 MB Parquet. Writing identical data plain CSV produces 2.1 GB file — 22 × larger.

text: DuckDB relational database engine designed analytical queries large tables. title: Parquet Files and DuckDB
text: Everything shown far used dplyr verbs dbplyr translating SQL behind scenes. Sometimes want write SQL directly — complex query cleaner SQL, debugging, simply comfortable . two levels can : inside R via DBI, outside R entirely. dbplyr::remote_con() exposes underlying DBI connection dr_con() object. DuckDB’s read_parquet() function lets reference Parquet file URL directly SQL, without needing know internal view name: can also open fresh DuckDB connection obus involvement : useful middle path: pass SQL string dplyr::tbl() get back lazy table can continue piping dplyr verbs onto: DuckDB ships standalone command-line executable. Parquet files just URLs; R Python installation needed: longer sessions, start interactive shell: three cases — R/DBI, CLI, Python — read_parquet() work: DuckDB reads file footer, applies filter chunk level, returns rows columns query needs.
code: con <- dbplyr::remote_con(dr_con("HL"))  DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "   SELECT   Survey, Year, COUNT(*) AS n_hauls   FROM     read_parquet('https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HL.parquet')   WHERE    Quarter = 1   GROUP BY Survey, Year   ORDER BY Survey, Year   LIMIT    12 ") Survey Year n_hauls 1    BITS 1991   16681 2    BITS 1992   14848 3    BITS 1993   17160 4    BITS 1994   18438 5    BITS 1995   20468 6    BITS 1996   23012 7    BITS 1997   19184 8    BITS 1998   20551 9    BITS 1999   25741 10   BITS 2000   25739 11   BITS 2001   23060 12   BITS 2002   22082 con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb())  DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "   SELECT   Survey,            SUM(NumberAtLength * SubsamplingFactor) AS n_total   FROM     read_parquet('https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HL.parquet')   WHERE    aphia = 126436   GROUP BY Survey   ORDER BY n_total DESC ")  DBI::dbDisconnect(con) tbl(con, sql("   SELECT .id, aphia, LengthClass, LengthCode, NumberAtLength, SubsamplingFactor   FROM   read_parquet('https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HL.parquet')   WHERE  Survey = 'NS-IBTS' AND Year = 2024 AND Quarter = 1 ")) |>   dr_add_length_cm() |>   filter(length_cm > 40) |>   collect() duckdb -c " SELECT   Survey, Year, COUNT(*) AS n_hauls FROM     read_parquet('https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HL.parquet') WHERE    Quarter = 1 GROUP BY Survey, Year ORDER BY Survey, Year LIMIT    12 " duckdb  D SELECT Survey, COUNT(*) AS n   FROM read_parquet('https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HL.parquet')   GROUP BY Survey ORDER BY n DESC; import duckdb con = duckdb.connect() con.execute("""     SELECT   Survey, Year, COUNT(*) AS n_hauls     FROM     read_parquet('https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HL.parquet')     WHERE    Quarter = 1     GROUP BY Survey, Year     ORDER BY n_hauls DESC     LIMIT    12 """).df()

what: The problem with raw DATRAS HL
title: Standardized HL: the semantic layer for DATRAS length data
text: DATRAS HL (length) table denormalized source record: contains length-frequency measurements haul-level bookkeeping. addition, value one variable often requires knowledge others external context: LengthClass value depends LengthCode (mm? 0.5 cm? cm?) NumberAtLength meaning depends DataType (raw count? CPUE? subsampled?) HaulDuration TotalNumber SpeciesCategoryWeight repeated every length row, creating redundancy aphia code; users think species names (latin common names). Supporting columns (LengthCode, SubsamplingFactor) implementation details extracted Users shouldn’t need understand interdependencies. work clean, self-contained data.

title: Standardized HL: the semantic layer for DATRAS length data
text: dr_standardize_hl() creates semantic layer — parquet file designed written reused across many analyses. also recommended starting point interactive work: filter(type == "length") output feeds directly dr_catch_by_haul() dr_expand_length() zero-filling, filter(type == "haul") gives haul-level numbers weights protocol metadata (StandardSpeciesCode, BycatchSpeciesCode) attached. See Catch products worked zero-fill examples. standardized HL parquet file : Self-contained: every variable unambiguous meaning. length_mm always millimetres. User-friendly: species names pre-joined. codes translate. Type-flagged: distinguishes length measurements haul bookkeeping Publication-ready: can served https reused many analyses
code: library(obus) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  hh <- dr_con("HH") hl <- dr_con("HL")  # Generate standardized HL for a small example hl_std <- dr_standardize_hl(   hh |> filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter == 1, Year == 2015, HaulValidity == "V") |> head(10),   hl |> filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Quarter == 1, Year == 2015) ) |> collect()  cat("Standardized HL rows:", nrow(hl_std), "\n") Standardized HL rows: 2106 cat("Type breakdown:\n") Type breakdown: print(table(hl_std$type)) haul length    266   1840

title: Standardized HL: the semantic layer for DATRAS length data
text: standardized HL type flag separates two fundamentally different data: p_females encodes sex composition single proportion: n_F / (n_F + n_M) computed fish recorded sex (sex ∈ {"F", "M", "B"} — "B", Berried/egg-bearing, known-female state counts female). NA fish group sexed (unsexed catches, "U"). Males females can recovered downstream: two types one table? Simplicity: users interact one parquet file Flexibility: users choose data use downstream Completeness: preserves caught species (even measured length) Zero-fill: type=“length” feeds dr_catch_by_haul() dr_expand_length() unchanged worked examples build survey indices top two row types — CPUE, length distributions, sex-specific indices using p_females — see Catch products.

title: Standardized HL: the semantic layer for DATRAS length data
text: Separation concerns: standardization (units, names, scaling) independent filtering/aggregation Reusability: one semantic layer, many downstream uses Reproducibility: everyone works standardized source Self-documenting: type flag makes contract explicit n_haul summed across type="length" rows (subsampling-corrected NumberAtLength) n_haul matching type="haul" row (TotalNumber) represent count agree within rounding: persistent, non-trivial mismatch feature data design — points inconsistency original DATRAS submission haul (TotalNumber reconciled underlying length measurements). dr_check_totalno() flags directly; practice affects roughly 1% records, almost small rounding differences DataType C. Redundancy eliminated — can’t reconstruct original HL type=“haul” rows alone (species/sex/category grouping lost) Inference required — need know fish went unmeasured, ’d need rejoin raw HL Trade-: smaller, cleaner file vs. lose granular subsampling details
code: length_total <- hl_std |>   filter(type == "length", .id == hl_std$.id[1]) |>   group_by(.id, species) |>   summarise(n_haul_from_length = sum(n_haul, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  haul_total <- hl_std |>   filter(type == "haul", .id == hl_std$.id[1]) |>   select(.id, species, n_haul)  full_join(length_total, haul_total, by = c(".id", "species")) |>   head(5) # A tibble: 5 × 4   .id                                  species         n_haul_from_length n_haul   <chr>                                <chr>                        <dbl>  <dbl> 1 NS-IBTS:2015:1:GB-SCT:748S:GOV:12:12 American plaice                161    161 2 NS-IBTS:2015:1:GB-SCT:748S:GOV:12:12 Atlantic cod                     4      4 3 NS-IBTS:2015:1:GB-SCT:748S:GOV:12:12 Atlantic herri…                  3      3 4 NS-IBTS:2015:1:GB-SCT:748S:GOV:12:12 Atlantic horse…                  9      9 5 NS-IBTS:2015:1:GB-SCT:748S:GOV:12:12 Atlantic macke…                  2      2

title: Standardized HL: the semantic layer for DATRAS length data
text: output carries two haul-level flags, StandardSpeciesCode BycatchSpeciesCode, document vessel required record. can substantially affect analysis depends species presence zero-hauls. See Sampling protocols article IMBUS documentation detailed treatment, including examples ignoring flags leads phantom zeros biased CPUE indices. exclude hauls incomplete recording zero-filling:
code: hl_std <- dr_standardize_hl(hh, hl)  complete_hauls <- hl_std |>   filter(type == "haul",          StandardSpeciesCode == "1",   # full standard species list          BycatchSpeciesCode  != "0")   # some bycatch recording done

title: Standardized HL: the semantic layer for DATRAS length data
text: dr_standardize_hl(hh, hl, species = NULL, haulval = NULL) takes: - hh: haul header table (DataType, HaulDuration, StandardSpeciesCode, BycatchSpeciesCode) - hl: length table (NumberAtLength, TotalNumber, etc.) - species: species lookup (defaults dr_con("species")) - haulval: optional haul validity filter (e.g. "V" ) Output: lazy DuckDB table 19 columns two row types. Can written parquet:
code: dr_standardize_hl(hh, hl) |>   dplyr::collect() |>   arrow::write_parquet("hl_standardized.parquet")

title: Standardized HL: the semantic layer for DATRAS length data

text: Rather importing, can open lazy DuckDB connection parquet files. Nothing downloaded explicitly call collect(): connection nearly instant — DuckDB reads file index, data. use ordinary dplyr verbs filter, join, compute; DuckDB translates SQL executes remote file. rows columns actually need travel wire: pipeline works unchanged whether hh hl DuckDB connections plain R data frames. can mix match — switch dr_get() need full table memory, use dr_con() want lazy filtering. dr_add_*() functions handle transparently. local parquet files, skip dr_con() use duckdbfs::open_dataset() directly — result identical: See Parquet DuckDB article full explanation Parquet, DuckDB, dbplyr fit together, including file format matters step--step walkthrough lazy query building.

title: Standardize HL into a clean catch foundation (length and haul summaries) — dr_standardize_hl
text: Produces unified catch table raw HH HL exchange tables two row types controlled type column:

title: Standardize HL into a clean catch foundation (length and haul summaries) — dr_standardize_hl
code: dr_standardize_hl(hh, hl, species = NULL, haulval = NULL)

title: Standardize HL into a clean catch foundation (length and haul summaries) — dr_standardize_hl
text: hh DATRAS HH table (standard column names, .id present). Required columns: .id, Survey, Year, Quarter, DataType, HaulDuration, StandardSpeciesCode, BycatchSpeciesCode. Optional: HaulValidity (used haulval set). hl DATRAS HL table (standard column names, .id present). Required type = "length": .id, aphia, NumberAtLength, LengthClass, LengthCode, SubsamplingFactor, sex, SpeciesValidity. Required type = "haul": additionally TotalNumber, SpeciesCategoryWeight, SpeciesCategory. species Species lookup columns aphia, latin, species. Defaults dr_con("species"). haulval Character vector HaulValidity codes retain. NULL keeps hauls.

title: Standardize HL into a clean catch foundation (length and haul summaries) — dr_standardize_hl
text: lazy DuckDB table columns: .id, Survey, Year, Quarter, aphia, latin, species, type, length_mm, length_cm, accuracy (NA type = "haul"), n_haul, n_hour, w_haul, w_hour (NA type = "length"), p_females, SpeciesValidity, StandardSpeciesCode, BycatchSpeciesCode.

title: Standardize HL into a clean catch foundation (length and haul summaries) — dr_standardize_hl
text: type = "length" One row per .id \(\times\) aphia \(\times\) length_mm. Sex DevelopmentStage collapsed; sex composition summarised p_females (proportion female among sexed fish). Derived NumberAtLength via dr_add_n_and_cpue. Replaces dr_catch_by_length(). type = "haul" One row per .id \(\times\) aphia. Numbers (n_haul, n_hour) come TotalNumber; weights (w_haul, w_hour) SpeciesCategoryWeight. Sex composition summarised p_females. Replaces dr_catch_total() (without zero-filling). Pass output dr_catch_by_haul zero-filling. Note: n_haul type = "haul" (TotalNumber) haul/species sum n_haul across type = "length" rows (subsampling-corrected NumberAtLength) represent count match within rounding. persistent mismatch indicates inconsistency original DATRAS submission, structural feature data; see dr_check_totalno.
